channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa’s Democracy: From Voting Rights to Real Freedom
item.description: As South Africa moves through mid-2026, the nation faces a sobering reality: political freedom has been secured, but socio-economic freedom remains uneven. Thirty-two years after 1994, voting rights h...

item.title: Trump Evacuated Safely After Security Incident at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump was safely evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner on Saturday after an armed individual attempted to breach security outside the ballroom at the ...

item.title: Sabastian Sawe breaks two-hour marathon barrier to win London Marathon in record time
item.description: Kenya’s Sabastian Sawe has delivered a historic performance at the London Marathon, becoming the first athlete to officially break the two-hour barrier in a competitive race. The 29-year-old clocked a...

item.title: A curated selection of stylish indoor and outdoor planters available in Canada
item.description: This Global News article highlights a curated selection of stylish planters currently available in Canada, aimed at enhancing both indoor and outdoor living spaces. The piece emphasizes how the right ...

item.title: O’Toole advises caution on China trade and expresses differing views from PM Carney on EV agreement
item.description: Former Conservative leader Erin O’Toole has commented on Canada’s economic and trade relations, emphasizing that China cannot serve as a replacement for the United States. In a recent statement, he ad...

item.title: Ottawa's Sunny and Warm Weather Continues Into Late April
item.description: Ottawa is experiencing a prolonged stretch of warm and sunny weather, with clear skies and mild temperatures continuing into late April. This stretch of good weather is welcomed by many in the city, e...

item.title: Trump commends Secret Service following White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting incident
item.description: During the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner held at the Washington Hilton, a serious security incident occurred when an armed suspect managed to reach a floor just above the ballroom whe...

item.title: Shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner Raises Concerns About Political Violence
item.description: During the White House Correspondents’ Association annual dinner at the Washington Hilton, a security incident occurred when an assailant armed with guns and knives attempted to breach a Secret Servic...

item.title: Indigenous Towns in Remote Australia Embrace Renewable Energy with Solar Microgrids
item.description: In remote north-western Australia, the Indigenous community of Djarindjin, home to nearly 400 Bardi and Jawi people, is taking steps to become energy self-sufficient. After experiencing frequent power...

item.title: Vilakazi criticises complacent Chippa United after heavy defeat to AmaZulu
item.description: Chippa United coach Vusimuzi Vilakazi was brutally honest following his team’s 5-1 loss to AmaZulu. The coach criticised his players for their complacency, stating that they had become 'too relaxed' d...

item.title: IVE reconnects with Filipino fans, showcasing growth and stronger stage presence
item.description: K-pop girl group IVE recently reunited with their Filipino fans, describing the experience as both meaningful and energizing. The group shared that they now feel “cooler” and more confident compared t...

item.title: Critique of Canada's High-Speed Rail Project Costs and Viability
item.description: The Globe and Mail editorial criticizes the proposed high-speed rail (HSR) project between Quebec City and Toronto, managed by Crown corporation Alto, for its exorbitant $60-billion to $90-billion pri...

item.title: Security Alert Declared in Northern Ireland Following Car Bomb Reports
item.description: A security alert has been declared in Northern Ireland after reports of a car bomb explosion, though further details are yet to emerge. The incident has heightened concerns in the region, as it comes ...

item.title: Multiple people injured after violent incident involving patient at Sydney hospital prompts safety concerns
item.description: A violent incident at Prince of Wales Hospital in Sydney has sparked renewed concern about safety in hospital settings, particularly within emergency departments. A 51-year-old male patient was arrest...

item.title: An exhibition at Kensington Palace explores the lives of the Duleep Singh princesses
item.description: An exhibition at Kensington Palace in London sheds light on the remarkable lives of the Duleep Singh sisters, the daughters of the last ruler of the Sikh Empire. These Anglo-Indian princesses, whose f...

item.title: Reclaiming Your Voice: Breaking Free from the Silence Imposed by Society
item.description: The article delves into the emotional journey of reclaiming one's voice, drawing from the powerful impact of the book 'ROAR!' by Marilise de Villiers Basson. It addresses how we are taught to remain s...

item.title: Trump calls off US delegation's visit to Pakistan amid Iran conflict talks
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Saturday announced the cancellation of the US delegation's visit to Pakistan, which was planned for peace talks concerning the ongoing conflict with Iran. Trump criticized...

item.title: Chelsea face Leeds in FA Cup semi-final under interim manager Calum McFarlane
item.description: The FA Cup semi-final at Wembley Stadium marks a pivotal moment for Chelsea as they prepare to face an in-form Leeds United side on 26 April 2026. The atmosphere surrounding the match is particularly ...

item.title: Southern Guild Opens New York Gallery to Showcase Contemporary African Art
item.description: Cape Town-based Southern Guild has opened a permanent gallery in New York's Tribeca district, marking a significant expansion and an important moment for contemporary African art on the global stage. ...

item.title: Academic Critique of J.K. Rowling's Views on Gender and Transphobia
item.description: Two Australian academics have published an article in the journal 'Celebrity Studies,' claiming that J.K. Rowling has transitioned from a global literary icon to embodying the divisive villain she onc...
